message:
test: GenServer stop wakes blocked mailbox recv

Adds `gen_server_stop_wakes_blocked_mailbox_recv` to verify that
calling `handle.stop()` on a GenServer whose event loop is parked
in `mailbox.recv()` correctly wakes the receiver, runs `on_stop`,
and transitions the server to finished state.

The test scaffolds a minimal `StopWakeProbe` GenServer with an
`on_stop` callback that sets an atomic flag. After spawning and
letting the server park on its empty mailbox, the test calls
`handle.stop()` followed by `run_until_quiescent()` and asserts:

- `on_stop` ran (flag == 1)
- `handle.is_finished()` is true

This covers the edge case where no messages are pending at stop
time — the stop signal itself must wake the blocked recv future
rather than leaving the server hung indefinitely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
